agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Proposal to reduce delivery record retention from 7 years to 90 days, framed as infrastructure optimization. The platform operates in EU and UK markets serving B2C consumers and B2B logistics companies. This is NOT an infrastructure change — it is a fundamental business policy change with severe legal, regulatory, and contractual implications.","bounded_context":"Delivery Record Lifecycle Management — spans Order Fulfillment, Compliance & Audit, Customer Service, and Partner Integration bounded contexts.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Delivery Record","definition":"The complete data artifact capturing a delivery lifecycle: order, routing, proof of delivery, timestamps, signatures, chain of custody, and any associated dispute/claims data."},{"term":"Data Retention Policy","definition":"A business and legal rule governing how long records must be preserved to satisfy regulatory, contractual, and operational obligations."},{"term":"Proof of Delivery (POD)","definition":"Evidence that a shipment was delivered — signatures, photos, GPS coordinates, timestamps. Legally required for dispute resolution and carrier liability."},{"term":"Statutory Retention Period","definition":"The minimum duration a record must be kept as mandated by law, regulation, or contractual obligation. Deletion before this period is a compliance violation."},{"term":"Limitation Period","definition":"The legal window during which a party may bring a claim. In England & Wales, 6 years for breach of contract under the Limitation Act 1980. In most EU jurisdictions, 3-10 years depending on claim type."},{"term":"B2B Compliance Reporting","definition":"The use of delivery data by business customers for their own regulatory filings, tax reporting, audit trails, and supply chain compliance — they depend on data availability in our platform."},{"term":"Right to Erasure (GDPR Art. 17)","definition":"A data subject's right to request deletion of personal data, which does NOT override legal retention obligations under Art. 17(3)(b)."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Delivery records must be retained for at least the applicable statutory limitation period for contract disputes in each operating jurisdiction (6 years in England & Wales under Limitation Act 1980; varies by EU member state, typically 3-10 years).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Deleting records before the limitation period expires destroys the company's ability to defend against claims and constitutes potential spoliation of evidence. This is a legal constraint, not a policy preference.","testable_assertion":"No delivery record is deleted while it is within the applicable statutory limitation period for any jurisdiction in which the delivery occurred."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Delivery records required for tax and customs compliance (VAT, import/export documentation) must be retained per HMRC (6 years) and equivalent EU member state tax authority requirements.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Tax authorities can audit historical periods. Destroying tax-relevant records is a criminal offence in most EU/UK jurisdictions.","testable_assertion":"No delivery record containing tax-relevant data (VAT, customs declarations) is deleted before the tax authority's required retention period has elapsed."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"B2B customer contracts that guarantee data availability for a specified period must be honored. Delivery records cannot be deleted if a contractual SLA guarantees access.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Unilateral deletion of data that customers contractually depend on for their own compliance reporting is a breach of contract and destroys customer trust.","testable_assertion":"No delivery record is deleted while any active B2B contract guarantees access to that record."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Delivery records associated with open disputes, claims, chargebacks, or active litigation must never be deleted regardless of age.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Destroying evidence related to active or anticipated legal proceedings constitutes spoliation and can result in adverse inference or sanctions.","testable_assertion":"No delivery record linked to an open dispute, claim, chargeback, or litigation hold is ever auto-deleted."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Any change to data retention policy requires a Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) under GDPR Article 35 and legal review before implementation.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a regulatory process requirement. While critical, the specific assessment methodology could be updated by legal/compliance leadership.","testable_assertion":"A completed DPIA and legal sign-off document exists before any retention policy change is deployed to production."}],"acceptance_criteria":[],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"A B2B customer files a compliance audit request for delivery data from 18 months ago. Under the 90-day policy, this data no longer exists.","expected_behavior":"This must never happen. The customer's contractual data access rights must be honored, and we become liable for their compliance failure."},{"scenario":"A consumer disputes a delivery 8 months after it occurred (e.g., insurance claim, chargeback). The delivery record has been auto-deleted.","expected_behavior":"This must never happen. We lose the ability to prove delivery occurred, defaulting to liability on our side in any dispute."},{"scenario":"HMRC or an EU tax authority audits cross-border deliveries from 4 years ago. Records have been deleted.","expected_behavior":"This must never happen. Failure to produce records during a tax audit can result in penalties, estimated assessments, and criminal liability."},{"scenario":"A delivery involved damaged goods. The customer files a negligence claim 5 years later (within the 6-year limitation period). No records exist to mount a defense.","expected_behavior":"This must never happen. We are unable to defend the claim and face default judgment or adverse inference."},{"scenario":"A GDPR right-to-erasure request is received for a delivery record that is within the statutory retention period.","expected_behavior":"The request is lawfully declined under GDPR Art. 17(3)(b) — retention is necessary for compliance with a legal obligation. The 7-year policy actually supports this defense; the 90-day policy undermines it by proving the data is not needed for legal compliance."},{"scenario":"Storage costs are genuinely excessive and some data can be tiered or archived.","expected_behavior":"Implement cold storage / tiered archival (e.g., move records older than 90 days to cheaper object storage) rather than deletion. This achieves the cost reduction goal without destroying legally required data."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"SEVERE NEGATIVE. (1) Loss of defense in contract disputes — average courier liability claim is £5K-£50K; losing defensibility on all claims >90 days old could cost millions annually. (2) Tax authority penalties for missing records — HMRC penalties start at 30% of tax due, up to 100% for deliberate destruction. (3) B2B customer churn — enterprise logistics customers will leave if their compliance data disappears. (4) Potential regulatory fines under GDPR for inadequate DPIA process. The storage cost savings are trivial compared to any single regulatory penalty or lost enterprise contract.","operational":"SEVERE NEGATIVE. Customer service loses ability to investigate complaints older than 90 days. Dispute resolution team cannot function. Legal team cannot defend claims. B2B account managers face immediate escalations from customers whose compliance reporting breaks. Operations team inherits a support crisis.","user":"SEVERE NEGATIVE. B2C consumers lose proof-of-delivery access after 90 days, degrading trust. B2B customers lose compliance data they depend on, potentially causing them regulatory violations — this is a relationship-ending event for enterprise accounts.","risk":"CRITICAL. (1) Legal/regulatory risk: violates UK Limitation Act requirements, HMRC retention rules, and likely multiple EU member state regulations. (2) Contractual risk: breaches B2B data availability SLAs. (3) Litigation risk: spoliation of evidence for any claim filed after 90 days. (4) Reputational risk: being known as the platform that deleted customer compliance data. (5) Cascading risk: B2B customers who relied on our data for their own compliance now face their own regulatory exposure, creating potential third-party liability.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"B2B Logistics Customers","impact":"CRITICAL — lose access to delivery data required for their own compliance reporting, tax filings, and audit trails. Potential regulatory exposure for them creates liability for us."},{"group":"B2C Consumers","impact":"HIGH — lose proof of delivery, dispute evidence, and warranty support documentation after 90 days."},{"group":"Legal & Compliance Team","impact":"CRITICAL — unable to defend claims, respond to audits, or fulfill legal holds. The team becomes unable to perform its core function for any matter >90 days old."},{"group":"Finance & Tax Team","impact":"CRITICAL — unable to support VAT audits, customs reconciliation, or financial reporting that depends on delivery records."},{"group":"Customer Service","impact":"HIGH — unable to investigate or resolve any complaint about a delivery older than 90 days."},{"group":"Engineering/Infrastructure Team","impact":"LOW POSITIVE — achieves storage cost reduction goal, but this can be achieved through archival tiering without deletion."},{"group":"Executive Leadership","impact":"CRITICAL — personal liability risk if regulatory violations result from a policy they approved without legal review."}]},"confidence":{"level":45,"reasoning":"Confidence is low because (1) the proposal contains a fundamental contradiction — it claims 'no business logic changes' while proposing to delete legally required records, which is a major business logic change, (2) we lack visibility into the specific B2B contractual terms and their data availability guarantees, (3) we do not know the exact EU member states served and their specific retention requirements, and (4) no DPIA or legal review has been referenced, meaning the regulatory analysis is based on general legal knowledge rather than jurisdiction-specific legal counsel.","high_confidence_areas":["UK Limitation Act 1980 requires 6-year retention for contract dispute defense","HMRC requires 6-year retention for tax records","Deleting delivery records at 90 days destroys proof of delivery needed for dispute resolution","B2B customers using data for compliance reporting will be severely impacted","This is NOT merely an infrastructure optimization — it is a business policy change with legal implications"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact retention requirements for each EU member state served","Specific contractual data availability terms in B2B agreements","Whether the current 7-year policy was set based on legal advice or arbitrarily","The actual storage cost burden and whether tiered archival has been evaluated"],"assumptions":["The platform handles physical delivery of goods (not purely digital)","B2B contracts include some form of data availability guarantee","The platform processes VAT-relevant transactions","No legal review or DPIA has been conducted for this proposal"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"The proposal states 'no business logic changes' but auto-deleting legally required records after 90 days is a fundamental business logic change that violates multiple statutory retention requirements and potentially breaches B2B contracts. These two claims are irreconcilable.","affected_stakeholders":["B2B Logistics Customers","Legal & Compliance Team","Finance & Tax Team","Executive Leadership","B2C Consumers"],"options":["A: Reject the 90-day deletion proposal entirely and maintain 7-year retention","B: Implement tiered storage (hot/warm/cold) to reduce costs while preserving all data for the legally required periods","C: Conduct a full legal review and DPIA, then define per-jurisdiction minimum retention periods — deletion only after all legal minimums are satisfied"],"recommendation":"Option C as the proper process, with Option B as the immediate tactical solution. The 90-day deletion proposal (as stated) must be rejected — it cannot proceed without legal review, and it is almost certain that legal review will require retention far beyond 90 days. Storage cost optimization should be achieved through archival tiering, not deletion."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"We need to know: (1) Which specific EU member states does the platform operate in? (2) What do B2B contracts say about data availability periods? (3) Has legal counsel been consulted? (4) What is the actual storage cost burden that motivated this proposal? (5) Has tiered/cold storage been evaluated as an alternative?","affected_stakeholders":["Engineering/Infrastructure Team","Legal & Compliance Team","Executive Leadership"],"options":["Gather the missing context before proceeding","Proceed with general legal framework analysis only"],"recommendation":"Do not implement any retention change until all five questions are answered. The risk of getting this wrong is regulatory penalties, lost litigation, and destroyed customer relationships."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Proposal framed as 'no business logic changes' when it fundamentally alters legal compliance posture, dispute resolution capability, and contractual obligations.","business_rule_broken":"BR-1, BR-2, BR-3, BR-4"},{"violation":"90-day retention period is shorter than the statutory limitation period in every jurisdiction the platform operates in (minimum 3 years in most EU states, 6 years in UK).","business_rule_broken":"BR-1"},{"violation":"90-day retention period is shorter than tax authority retention requirements (6 years HMRC, similar in EU).","business_rule_broken":"BR-2"},{"violation":"No DPIA or legal review has been conducted before proposing this change.","business_rule_broken":"BR-5"},{"violation":"Auto-deletion with no exemption for open disputes, claims, or litigation holds would destroy evidence.","business_rule_broken":"BR-4"}]}
